concept: Moss / Soft Ground Cover / Ancient Green
description: Small, soft plants that grow in dense clumps on rocks, trees, and moist ground. 'Shumu' names the quiet ancients—older than flowering plants, thriving in shade and damp, creating soft green cushions that hold moisture and time.
sound_symbolism: The word cushions softly. 'Shu' /ʃu/ begins with hushed softness, the yielding surface. Then 'mu' muffles with nasal closure, sound absorbed into the moss bed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'shumu' (moss), usually as a mass noun for coverage. A counted living moss growth may take optional 'lipha'; a patch as an area has no canonical classifier. Associated with age, moisture, shade, and quiet places.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Moss offered Art Nouveau artists texture and the beauty of the overlooked—soft green life thriving in shadow and crack.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Moss indicated water sources, provided wound dressing, and insulated dwellings—ancient knowledge of this humble plant's gifts.
